notes: Both configurations 5/5 — this eval's assertions are not discriminating enough. The task was narrow enough for baseline to handle without skill.

article-count-check passes 5/5 in both configurations — assertions are not discriminating enough for this narrow, focused task. In iteration 2, add assertions that test structured PASS/FAIL output and deeper checks.
Skill saves ~136s average per run (with_skill 216s vs without_skill 352s) — structured templates reduce exploratory tool use significantly.

missing-script shows the biggest improvement: 1.0 vs 0.2. The skill's domain knowledge (llama3.2:3b JSON malformation, _clean_json, USE_LOCAL_LLM fallback) cannot be derived from code inspection without MEMORY.md access.

full-quality-check: with_skill 9 tool calls vs without_skill 8 — almost identical tool usage but radically different output quality (5/5 vs 1/5). The skill's structured template, not more reading, drives the improvement.

newsflow-add-parser: without_skill took 32 tool calls on et-ai-parser vs 21 with_skill — baseline rewrote the parser when it should have just validated it, wasting effort and modifying production code unnecessarily.

low-article-count: without_skill 4/5 — strong baseline, close to skill performance. The TLDR weekend publishing schedule is the key differentiator. Add this to the skill's Ingestion failures section more prominently.

Hallucination risk: without_skill baseline fabricated log contents and non-existent parameters (expansion_mode) in missing-script. The skill's checkpoint-first methodology grounds Claude in real data.
